backlog.name: loop-strength-reduction
backlog.note: Struct index recomputation in doCompact loop; i*48 each iter vs increment

backlog.name: bytes-compare-less-pattern
backlog.note: Compare(a,b)==-1 -> Compare(a,b)<0 saves 1 byte codegen; source fix simpler

backlog.name: pgo-block-layout
backlog.note: Profile-guided block placement for branch prediction

backlog.name: loop-unrolling
backlog.note: Walk/doCompact inner loops could benefit from unrolling

last_run.notes: Explored non-SSA optimizations per user request. GOAMD64=v3 showed no significant improvement. Identified 7 non-SSA optimization opportunities. Main bottleneck is indirect call overhead (20% of time) in generics dictionary dispatch - not fixable without ABI changes.

key_findings: SSA optimization frontier exhausted for this workload
Non-SSA frontier also largely exhausted - main bottleneck is Go ABI (all caller-saved regs)
Indirect call in items.find (CALL CX) = 20% of time, 7 spills per call, not devirtualizable
cmpbody = 10% flat, hand-written AVX2 asm, already optimal for 64-byte keys
GOAMD64=v3 eliminates runtime AVX2 check but no measurable benchmark improvement
No loop strength reduction in Go - struct index i*48 recomputed each iteration
No loop unrolling in Go compiler
bytes.Compare(a,b)==-1 generates CMPQ+SETEQ; <0 would generate TESTQ+SETLT (1 byte smaller)
Layout pass is basic topological sort, no PGO-guided block placement
Remaining bounds checks in walk (reverse iteration) not eliminable by prove pass
Go ABI has NO callee-saved GP registers - fundamental spill cost around every call
